skill.name: ui-ux-pro-max
skill.description: Comprehensive UI/UX design intelligence with 50+ styles, 21 color palettes, 50 font pairings, 20 chart types, and 9 technology stacks (React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, Tailwind, shadcn/ui). Provides actionable design recommendations, accessibility guidelines, and component patterns.
skill.summary: Complete design system reference covering styles, colors, typography, charts, and component guidelines across 9 major tech stacks.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 1176 patterns but nearly all are false positives. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are hex color codes (#2563EB) in design data files. 'Hardcoded URLs' are documentation links in guidelines. 'System reconnaissance' and 'C2 keywords' are design terminology matches. Python scripts read CSV files to generate design recommendations - no network calls or code execution. One legitimate concern: scripts/design_system.py uses subprocess calls (Ruby/shell execution patterns) for design tooling integration, which is appropriate for this use case but documented as a minor risk.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution in Design Scripts
medium_findings.description: scripts/design_system.py contains shell command execution patterns for design tooling integration. This is legitimate for a design system generator but represents a minor risk if the script were modified to accept untrusted input.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Shell execution is present but uses hardcoded commands for design tooling - no user input injection vectors identified in current implementation.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs in Design Guidelines
low_findings.description: CSV data files contain documentation URLs (e.g., https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/) as part of design guidelines. These are reference links, not network calls made by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are documentation references stored in CSV data fields, not fetch() or HTTP client calls executed at runtime.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hex Color Codes Flagged as Crypto
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged hex color codes (e.g., #2563EB, #3B82F6) as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. These are design color values, not cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern match on hex strings (#XXXXXX format) is correct but semantic context confirms these are CSS color values in a design system, not cryptographic keys or hashes.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Terminology Flagged as Suspicious
low_findings.description: Terms like 'reconnaissance' (in UX research context), 'keylogger' (in security UI guidelines), and 'C2' (likely 'call-to-action' abbreviation) triggered keyword detectors but are legitimate design domain vocabulary.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Domain-specific terminology in UX guidelines - 'reconnaissance' refers to user research, security terms appear in accessibility guideline examples.

content.user_title: Build Professional UI/UX Designs with AI Guidance
content.value_statement: Designers and developers struggle to maintain consistent, accessible design systems across projects. This skill provides comprehensive design guidelines, color palettes, typography recommendations, and component patterns across 9 major technology stacks.
actual_capabilities: Generate color palette recommendations for 21+ product types (SaaS, e-commerce, healthcare, fintech) with accessible contrast ratios
Provide component guidelines for 9 tech stacks (React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, Tailwind, shadcn/ui)
Recommend typography pairings from 50+ font combinations with appropriate sizing for headings and body text
Suggest chart types from 20+ visualization options based on data characteristics and use case
Deliver accessibility checklists with WCAG compliance guidance (color contrast, focus states, keyboard navigation)
Generate UX guidelines for specific contexts (landing pages, dashboards, mobile apps, forms, overlays)
limitations: Provides design guidance and recommendations only - does not generate actual visual mockups or design files
Guidelines are based on established best practices but may not account for brand-specific design requirements
Cannot replace user testing or accessibility auditing tools for production validation
Technology stack guidelines reflect common patterns but may not cover newest framework versions

use_cases.title: Startup Building MVP Design System
use_cases.description: A solo founder uses the skill to establish consistent UI patterns for their SaaS product, selecting appropriate color palettes, component styles, and accessibility guidelines for their React-based application.

use_cases.title: Design Team Standardizing Components
use_cases.description: A design team lead leverages the comprehensive guidelines to create a unified design system documentation, ensuring all team members follow consistent patterns across multiple product lines.
use_cases.target_user: Design team leads and UX managers

use_cases.title: Developer Implementing Accessibility Requirements
use_cases.description: A frontend developer references the accessibility guidelines to ensure their Vue application meets WCAG 2.1 requirements, including proper color contrast, focus management, and keyboard navigation patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Color Palette Selection
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm building a [product type, e.g., SaaS dashboard]. Recommend a color palette with primary, secondary, CTA, background, text, and border colors. Include hex codes and explain the color psychology.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick color selection for new projects

prompt_templates.title: Component Implementation Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me best practices for implementing [component, e.g., data tables] in [framework, e.g., React]. Include accessibility requirements, common patterns, and code examples.

prompt_templates.title: Accessibility Audit Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my [page/component type] for accessibility issues. I have [describe your UI]. What WCAG guidelines should I check? Provide a prioritized checklist.

prompt_templates.title: Complete Design System Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a comprehensive design system for a [product type] targeting [audience]. Include: color palette, typography scale, component styles, spacing system, and UX guidelines specific to my domain.

output_examples.input: Recommend a color palette for a healthcare app
output_examples.output: Healthcare App Color Palette: Primary (#0891B2 - Calm Cyan) establishes trust and calmness. Secondary (#22D3EE - Light Cyan) provides visual hierarchy. CTA (#059669 - Success Green) for primary actions. Background (#ECFEFF - Soft White) reduces eye strain. Text (#164E63 - Deep Teal) ensures readability. Border (#A5F3FC - Light Border) for subtle separation. This palette prioritizes calmness and trust, critical for healthcare applications where users may experience anxiety.

output_examples.input: What are the touch target requirements for mobile buttons?
output_examples.output: Touch Target Guidelines: Minimum 44x44 pixels (iOS HIG and WCAG requirement). Recommended 48x48 pixels for frequently-used actions. Padding should be included in the measurement, not just the visible button. Spacing between touch targets: minimum 8px to prevent accidental taps. For critical actions (delete, purchase), consider 56x56px minimum.

best_practices: Always verify color contrast ratios meet WCAG AA minimum (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text) before finalizing designs
Maintain consistency across all pages - use the same style, color palette, and component patterns throughout your application
Design mobile-first: start with touch-friendly layouts (44px+ targets) and scale up for larger screens
Use semantic color names (primary, secondary, error) instead of hardcoded values to enable theming and dark mode support
Test designs with reduced motion preferences (prefers-reduced-motion) to ensure accessibility for users with vestibular disorders
anti_patterns: Using emoji as icons in production interfaces - they render inconsistently across platforms and lack professional appearance
Implementing hover-only interactions without tap/click alternatives - excludes touch device users and accessibility scenarios
Skipping loading states and skeleton screens - creates perceived performance issues and user uncertainty during async operations
Hardcoding colors inline instead of using design tokens or CSS variables - prevents theming and creates maintenance burden
Using animation for decorative purposes without respecting prefers-reduced-motion - can cause discomfort or nausea for some users

faq.question: Does this skill work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill is compatible with all three AI assistants. Each can access the design guidelines and provide recommendations based on your specific project needs.

faq.question: Can I use these guidelines for commercial projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is licensed under MIT, which permits commercial use. The guidelines reference industry-standard best practices from established design systems.

faq.question: How do I adapt these guidelines to my existing brand colors?
faq.answer: Use the color psychology and contrast recommendations while substituting your brand's primary colors. The guidelines provide ratios and relationships that work with any base color.

faq.question: Are these accessibility guidelines WCAG compliant?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidelines reference WCAG 2.1 AA standards for color contrast, touch targets, focus states, and keyboard navigation. Always verify with automated testing tools for production.

faq.question: Which technology stack should I choose for my project?
faq.answer: The skill provides guidelines for 9 stacks. React/Next.js for web apps, React Native/SwiftUI/Flutter for mobile, Vue/Svelte for lightweight web, Tailwind for utility-first CSS, and shadcn/ui for Radix-based components. Consider your team's expertise and project requirements.

faq.question: How often are the design guidelines updated?
faq.answer: Design best practices evolve over time. The skill contains established patterns from major design systems (Material Design, Human Interface Guidelines, Carbon, etc.) that remain relevant across framework versions.
